headline: The Top 20% Own Nearly Everything
context: DOSM's 2023 Household Income Survey shows the top 20% of Malaysian households control approximately 70% of national wealth while the bottom 40% hold under 8%.

cards.big: Top 20% hold 70% of wealth. Bottom 40% hold under 8%.
cards.sub: Malaysia's Gini coefficient for wealth: 0.67 — higher than income inequality suggests.

cards.big: EPF data: 72% of members have under RM50,000 at age 55.
cards.sub: The median Malaysian retires into poverty. The average is pulled up by the top decile.

cards.big: NEP redistributed income. It did not redistribute wealth.
cards.sub: Asset ownership concentration increased across all ethnic groups since 1990.

cards.big: Property: top 10% hold 45% of residential real estate.
cards.sub: Affordable housing quotas address flow. The stock problem is untouched.

cards.big: Income policy cannot fix a wealth problem.
cards.sub: When the conversation stays on salaries, the balance sheet stays hidden.

cards.big: Like a 10-storey apartment block where two floors hold 70% of the furniture — the other eight floors share what is left
cards.sub: Wealth concentration means most Malaysians compete for a shrinking share of national prosperity

cards.big: Malaysia debates income. The crisis is wealth. Until the metric changes, the policy will keep missing.
